$comment: Generative news-article corpus for the 1970s (glamour-and-grit motorsport-weekly voice — warm, novelistic, present-tense print race reports, cigarette-livery money and jet-set danger, wings and ground-effect arriving late-decade), parsed by Companion.Core.News.NewsArticleBank. Keys under 'bodies' are 'journalPhase|cause'; each holds era-keyed body templates selected deterministically via the 'headlines' PCG32 stream. Bodies compose literal prose, fact tokens, and {pool:name} references. Fact tokens: {player} {team} {race} {year} {round} {position} {expected} {winner} {fieldSize} {champPosition} {champMove} {champLeader}.

1970s: From the paddock at {race}, our correspondent writes.
A dispatch from {race}, in the season of {year}.
Trackside at {race}, where the sponsors' colours blaze.
Filed from the pit lane at {race}, {year}.
By our man among the transporters and the money.
In the motorhomes tonight the champagne is on ice, and the season sings.
The paddock, all money and glamour, has found itself a new favourite.
One for the scrapbooks, and for the backers who paid handsomely to be there.
The crowd streams away warmed by what they have seen.
A drive to set the turnstiles clicking at the next round.
In the transporter there are long faces and longer silences.
The sponsors' guests drift away, their champagne gone flat.
A weekend the team will want washed clean from the record.
There is racing yet to come, and the paddock is nothing if not forgiving.
The stopwatch, cruel and honest, keeps its own counsel.
default: The team looks ahead to the next round.

1970s: In the title chase {player} now stands {champPosition}, {champMove} on the campaign.
The championship ledger reads {player} {champPosition} — {champMove} as the season unfolds.
That leaves {player} {champPosition} in the hunt for the crown, {champMove}, with {champLeader} the man to beat.
{player} sits {champPosition} in the running for the title, {champMove}.
from a car the pundits had pencilled in for no better than {expected}
against a machine reckoned worth only {expected}
having been fancied for nothing grander than {expected}
the cigarette livery gleaming under the lights
backers' names bright along the flanks
the sponsors' colours worn like a matador's cape
the money men on the pit wall smiling for once
the bodywork ablaze with the season's richest names
default: the team colours out in force
the sponsors watching from the pit wall

1970s: the new wings biting hard into the corners
downforce pressing the car into the road as never before
the ground-effect skirts doing their quiet, ferocious work
the aerodynamics rewriting what a corner might be taken at
default: the machine well and truly sorted

1970s: The transporters roll for home now, and a long summer of racing folds gently into the record and the memory.
In the motorhomes the corks are pulled and the sponsors' guests linger late, for a season like this one comes but rarely.
The paddock empties, the money men tally their winnings, and already the talk drifts toward next spring and all it might hold.
default: The season is run, and the paddock turns its face toward winter.
So closes another year of racing, and another begins to take shape.

1970s: {pool:dateline} {player} takes {race} for {team}, a victory built lap by imperious lap and driven home ahead of a field of {fieldSize}. The {year} win comes with {pool:sponsorLine}, and no one in the paddock argues its merit. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} There is a winner at {race}, and it is {player}. From flag to flag the {team} car is never truly headed, {pool:techNote}, and the grandstands rise as one at the finish. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} Victory, glorious and complete, for {player} at {race}. Round {round} of {year} belongs to {team} outright — the sort of drive that sends photographers scrambling and sponsors reaching for their cheque-books. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
default: {player} wins {race} for {team}, leading home a field of {fieldSize}. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

1970s: {pool:dateline} A place on the rostrum for {player}, home {position} at {race} and drenched in champagne for the trouble. {team} split the leaders in a field of {fieldSize}, with {winner} first to the flag. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} {player} carries {team} onto the podium at {race}, {position} at the fall of the flag with {pool:sponsorLine}. Only {winner} finished ahead. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} {position} and a podium for {player} at {race}, a drive {pool:expectationBeat} that had the pit wall on its feet. {winner} won the day, but the afternoon belonged to {team} as much as anyone. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} {player} brings the {team} car home {position} at {race}, honest points banked from a field of {fieldSize}. {winner} took the spoils, but the ledger ticks over nicely. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} No champagne, but no complaints: {player} scores at {race}, {position} at the flag and the {team} account the healthier for it. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} They will talk of this one for weeks: {player} hauls the {team} machine to {position} at {race} {pool:expectationBeat}, {pool:techNote} and a right foot that would not be denied. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} The drive of the afternoon, by common consent, is {player}'s — {position} at {race} from a field of {fieldSize}, {pool:expectationBeat}, and the whole paddock knows it. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} A flat afternoon for {player}, only {position} at {race} where {expected} had seemed the least of it. {team} will pore over the timing sheets long into the night. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}

{pool:dateline} The pace simply never came for {player} at {race}, home {position} in a field of {fieldSize} while {winner} disappeared up the road. A weekend {team} would rather not dwell upon. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}
default: {player} was below par at {race}, only {position} when {expected} was the hope. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}

1970s: {pool:dateline} Cruelty of the mechanical sort at {race}: the {team} car lets {player} down while running strong, no blame at all to the driver, from an afternoon that had promised {expected}. {winner} took the win. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} Smoke, then silence, and {player} is out at {race} with the machine broken beneath him. The romance of the sport turns hard on days like these. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} {player} goes off at {race} and is out on the spot — a moment's misjudgement that leaves {team} to tally the cost. {winner} led home a field of {fieldSize}. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} A grand chance thrown away: {player} puts the {team} car into the scenery at {race}, out where {expected} had beckoned so warmly only laps before. {pool:closeBad}
default: {player} crashed out of {race} through driver error. {pool:closeBad}

1970s: {pool:dateline} A quiet, professional afternoon for {player}, {position} at {race} in a field of {fieldSize}. Little for the front pages, but the miles are logged and the {team} car is home intact. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} No headlines for {player} at {race}, {position} at the flag and some way behind {winner}. An unremarkable day amid the glamour for {team}. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
default: {player} ran to {position} at {race}, a quiet afternoon for {team}. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

1970s: The season of {year} is run to its very last mile, and it is {player} who stands atop it all — champion of the world, the {team} name gilded now in the record book for as long as men keep such things. It has been a long and glorious summer of racing, of glamour and grease and money by the fistful, and every hard-won yard of it belonged, in the end, to this. {pool:seasonClose}

Champions are not made in a single afternoon but forged across the whole long arc of a season, and the {year} crown, richly and unarguably, is {player}'s. The {team} motorhome will toast this one for years yet, the sponsors' colours never worn more proudly than they are tonight. {pool:seasonClose}
default: {player} is champion of {year}, a title season for {team}. {pool:seasonClose}

1970s: The {year} championship is settled at the last, and it is {champLeader} who carries off the crown, {player} classified {champPosition} at the season's close for {team}. There is no shame in a year run hard and honest, and the winter now offers time enough to sharpen the blade for the campaign to come. {pool:seasonClose}

So the long reckoning of {year} is complete: {champLeader} stands champion of the world, {player} closing the year {champPosition} for {team}. The season had its glories and its cruelties both, as every season must, and already the paddock's thoughts are drifting toward next spring. {pool:seasonClose}
default: {champLeader} took the {year} title; {player} finished the year {champPosition} for {team}. {pool:seasonClose}
